title: ranunculus occidentalis subsp. nelsonii
intro: Ranunculus occidentalis subsp. nelsonii, also known as Nelson's buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Ranunculus. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The roots of Ranunculus occidentalis subsp. nelsonii have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, but its preparation and dosage should be done under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
